topics: Minimum voting age in congregational meetings
synopsis: The SJC sustained Mr. Timothy Psiaki's complaint that Covenant Presbyterian Church (Issaquah, WA) erred in excluding communicant members under age 18 from voting in the election of church officers. The court held that BCO 6-4 entitles all communicant members to voting rights in congregational elections, and that the congregation's bylaws imposing an age restriction violated the Constitution. Multiple dissenting opinions argued that the phrase 'good and regular standing' permits local congregations to regulate voting based on age and maturity.
